note_html:
Here is the SOAP note based on the transcript:

Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Charles, presented with a complaint of shortness of breath, stating that he feels like he catches his breath a lot more than he used to, even considering himself to be pretty healthy. He reported that the symptoms started suddenly, and he is not sure why. He denied any other symptoms such as a rash, nausea, vomiting, lip or throat swelling, or fever. He mentioned that he has a dog that gets eye discharge, but he himself does not experience eye discharge. He reported that the symptoms occur in all situations, including when he is in the house, outside, and near his cat.

Past medical history: The patient reported that he has a history of heavy drinking, which leads to diarrhea.

Review of systems: The patient denied any symptoms in other organs, but reported that he has noticed the shortness of breath in all situations.

Current medications: The patient is currently using an albuterol inhaler as prescribed by the urgent care for asthma symptoms.

Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs are normal, with a normal pulse and pulse ox.

Physical exam: The patient's physical exam was notable for faint expiratory wheezing bilaterally in all lung fields. There was no rash, angioedema, or audible stridor.

Test Results: The patient's chest X-ray and pulmonary function test were normal.

Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has newly diagnosed allergic asthma.

Problem list: Allergic asthma; suspected allergy; unknown past medical history; unknown social history.

Plan:
I prescribed albuterol inhaler as directed for asthma symptoms. I prescribed Singulair 10mg once daily to help decrease the occurrences of asthma. I also plan to proceed with allergy testing, starting with skin testing to target the triggers of the patient's symptoms. We will schedule the skin testing for next week. I want the patient to continue using the albuterol inhaler as soon as symptoms start and to come to the office next week for the scheduled appointment.

Follow-up: I will see the patient next week to schedule the skin testing.
